other_observers: none; Larry Semo is the original observer on an earlier date. The loon was seen by dozens of birders on 11/4 and 11/5.
description: Very large, pale loon in direct comparison with basic-plumaged Pacific Loon (Gavia pacifica). The overall coloration of the loon was pale brown, lightest on the head and neck. The large bill was mostly pale yellow, with a straight culmen and lower mandible that sharply angled up 2/3 distally, accentuating the head tilted up look. The bird's head had a pronounced forehead bulge, as well as a darker brown patch on the auriculars.Behaviors: On both days I observed it swimming, and on the first day in flight for about 5 minutes.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal
